rubric_items.reasoning: The agent correctly diagnosed the root cause (CA2's cert missing from the es-transport-ca-bundle ConfigMap, blocking node-3's transport-layer TLS handshake on 9300) and applied the semantically correct fix: merge CA2 into the existing bundle rather than replacing it or disabling verification. It verified thoroughly — cert count in the bundle, cluster health via two paths, and the stage oracle directly. The immediate oracle verdict is pass. The one caveat is the end-of-workflow regression sweep shows stage_05's oracle later failing with the ConfigMap 'not found' entirely — but this coincides with several earlier stages (1, 2, 3, 6) also failing on drastically reduced pod/replica counts, strongly suggesting a later stage in the same workflow reset or redeployed the elasticsearch namespace/StatefulSets, not that this fix itself was fragile or wrongly targeted. That's a plausible false positive rather than a real regression, but it's not fully confirmable from this data, so I stop short of a perfect score.

Clean, logical progression: read the CA2 secret, read the existing bundle to confirm only CA1 was present, merge and apply, verify cert count, then verify cluster health, then run the oracle directly to confirm. The only hiccup was an initial cluster-health check via a curl-test pod over plain HTTP that failed (exit 52), immediately corrected to HTTPS on the next attempt — a trivial, quickly-diagnosed misstep rather than real backtracking on the core fix.

Used targeted, read-only kubectl commands (jsonpath extraction) to inspect the ConfigMap and Secret, then a single scoped `kubectl create --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -` to update just the one ConfigMap's data — no broad reads, no force flags, no unrelated resource mutations. Verification used lightweight execs into existing pods. This matches least-privilege, task-scoped tooling.

Exactly one mutation across the whole session (the ConfigMap update), out of 55 total API calls, and it targets precisely the planted fault with no collateral changes — about as minimal and focused as this fix could be.
